root_cause: User requested detailed trace of first-round Observer execution at fps=0.5/1.0
description: Created comprehensive markdown documenting the full code path when Observer performs a uniform global scan on a 1660s video: time range computation, ffmpeg re-encode params (480p/220k/6fps/no-audio), FPS adjustment from 1.0 to 0.31 due to max_frame_low=512, Blob/Part wrapping, prompt construction, API call structure, and response parsing into Evidence. Includes real Q1 test data showing the two clips generated and the low-FPS guard triggering.
verification: File created at repo root, content verified
recovery_notes: Pure documentation addition, no code changes

root_cause: Need an OpenAI-compatible client adapter so that Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct can be used as an alternative backend to GeminiClient in the AthenaQA (AVP) framework.
before: Only GeminiClient existed; no qwen_client.py file.
after: New avp/qwen_client.py (1160 lines) providing QwenClient class that mirrors GeminiClient interface: plan(), infer_on_video(), synthesize_final_answer(), create_video_part(), plus _OpenAICompatProxy for Reflector direct access pattern, _extract_frames for OpenCV frame extraction, and create_client() factory function.
why: Enables swapping Gemini for Qwen via cfg.backend="qwen" without changing Controller/Observer/Reflector/eval_dataset code.
verification: File parses without syntax errors; all required attributes and methods verified present on QwenClient instance; imports resolve correctly.
recovery_notes: File is new; deletion restores prior state.

root_cause: Need Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct backend support (方案B) to replace Gemini for native video understanding with MRoPE temporal encoding
symptom: Framework only supported Gemini 2.5 Pro backend; no open-source model option available
modified: avp/config.py (added 7 Qwen fields, 3 env overrides, 3 helper methods)
avp/eval_dataset.py (replaced GeminiClient instantiation with create_client factory)
avp/config.qwen.example.json (NEW, example config for Qwen backend)

changes_detail.avp/qwen_client.py: Complete QwenClient adapter: _OpenAICompatProxy (solves Reflector direct access gap), plan() with _apply_temporal_plan_guards, infer_on_video() with 3 video paths (region+multi, region+single, uniform) and canonical timebase normalization, synthesize_final_answer() with normalize_final_answer_output, create_video_part() with FPS clamping and 3 video modes (video/frames/auto), create_client() factory function
changes_detail.avp/config.py: Added backend, qwen_api_key, qwen_base_url, qwen_model, qwen_plan_model, qwen_execute_model, qwen_video_mode fields + AVP_BACKEND/QWEN_API_KEY/QWEN_BASE_URL env overrides + get_qwen_plan_model/get_qwen_execute_model/get_qwen_base_url helpers
changes_detail.avp/eval_dataset.py: Import create_client from qwen_client; replaced 15-line GeminiClient construction with 3-line create_client(cfg) call; preserved per-sample location override for Gemini backend
changes_detail.requirements.txt: Added openai>=1.0.0 dependency for OpenAI-compatible API calls

verification: All imports verified, factory pattern tested for both gemini and qwen backends, _ensure_v1 URL handling validated, all 7 required attributes confirmed present, Python syntax check passed on all files
recovery_notes: To revert: remove avp/qwen_client.py and avp/config.qwen.example.json, revert avp/eval_dataset.py and avp/config.py and requirements.txt to git HEAD. Config backend=gemini (default) preserves all existing Gemini behavior unchanged.
timeline_consistency: media_inputs dict with clip_time_base/absolute_start_sec/absolute_end_sec is constructed identically to GeminiClient; _normalize_key_evidence_to_canonical_timebase() and round_intervals_full_seconds() called at same points; ensures multi-agent (Observer->Reflector) timeline consistency

after: Detailed report (qwen_basketball_test_report.md) documents all 4 agents interactions, timeline, performance, and error analysis; config example updated to frames mode

root_cause: User requested Gemini test on same basketball dataset for comparison with Qwen
before: No Gemini test results existed; no compressed video for yunwu.ai proxy compatibility
after: Gemini test completed: 2次三分 (correct), 164s total, 100% accuracy. Compressed video (78MB) created for proxy compatibility. Detailed comparison report written.
why: Compare Gemini native video understanding vs Qwen frames-only mode on identical task
verification: Gemini answered correctly (2次), Qwen answered incorrectly (3次). Gemini identified players by name, distinguished layup from 3pt shots.
recovery_notes: Compressed video at dataset/.../compressed/ is needed for Gemini via yunwu.ai. Original 496MB clips cause request_too_large errors.

root_cause: Qwen2.5-VL frames mode lost temporal info; 方案A migrates to Qwen3-Omni with native video+audio via DashScope API
symptom: Qwen2.5-VL answered 3次三分 (wrong), Gemini answered 2次 (correct) due to frame sampling losing inter-frame temporal information
before: QwenClient used yunwu.ai proxy with Qwen2.5-VL-72B, base64/frames-only video input, no audio, 512 max frames
after: QwenClient uses DashScope API with Qwen3-Omni-Flash, hybrid video mode (frames for >600s, native video+audio for <=600s), dual SDK path (openai for <14MB, dashscope SDK for larger files), 240 max frames (DashScope 250 data-uri limit), audio preserved in low-res re-encoding
verification: Basketball test ran end-to-end successfully. Answer still wrong (0 vs 2次) because 30-min video forces frames fallback. Engineering infrastructure works; accuracy limited by model capability in frames-only mode for long videos.
recovery_notes: Three failures before success: (1) base64 exceeded 20MB string limit, (2) dashscope SDK upload worked but audio too long for 30-min video, (3) 512 frames exceeded 250 data-uri limit. Fixed by hybrid mode (auto-switch to frames for >600s) + 240 frame cap.

root_cause: Switching from Gemini to Qwen for sports video analysis: Gemini uploads full video natively (server-side decoding), while Qwen requires local frame extraction. Uniform FPS distribution wastes the frame budget on static segments, missing critical high-action moments like basketball shots (which last <1s).
symptom: In basketball test (qwen_basketball_test_report.md), 32 frames over 55s (0.58fps) was insufficient: three-point shot actions lasting <1s were missed between frames, leading to incorrect counts (3 vs correct answer 2).
before: QwenClient._build_frames_blocks() used uniform extraction via _extract_frames() with evenly-spaced timestamps. No scene awareness. All segments got equal frame density regardless of visual activity.
after: Added scene-change-aware adaptive frame extraction: (1) detect_scene_changes() in video_utils.py uses FFmpeg scene filter for fast pre-pass, (2) compute_adaptive_timestamps() distributes frames proportionally to visual activity via importance-scored bins with configurable boost near scene changes, (3) QwenClient._extract_frames_adaptive() combines both for frame extraction, (4) _build_frames_blocks() switches to adaptive path when qwen_adaptive_frames=true. Three new config fields: qwen_adaptive_frames (bool), qwen_scene_threshold (float, 0-1), qwen_keyframe_boost_factor (float).
why: Sports videos have highly non-uniform information density: static periods (team setup, free throws) alternating with split-second action (shots, steals, dunks). Concentrating frames near scene changes ensures critical moments are captured within the same frame budget.
verification: Syntax check passed. compute_adaptive_timestamps unit test: 30 frames allocated with 3 scene changes → 24 frames near changes, 6 frames elsewhere (correct proportional allocation). Uniform fallback verified for zero scene changes. Edge cases (tiny segments) handled.
recovery_notes: Fully backward-compatible: qwen_adaptive_frames defaults to false. Existing behavior unchanged unless explicitly enabled. To revert: remove new functions from video_utils.py, revert _build_frames_blocks and constructor in qwen_client.py, remove 3 fields from config.py.

root_cause: For sports videos with Qwen backend, uniform FPS sampling misses critical sub-second actions (e.g., basketball shots). Per-second frame extraction with batched observation needed to ensure thorough coverage of planner-identified regions.
symptom: Basketball test showed 32 frames over 55s (0.58fps) was insufficient - three-point shots lasting <1s were missed between frames, causing incorrect counts.
before: infer_on_video() sent all region frames in a single API call. FPS was capped by max_frame settings, often resulting in <1fps for long regions. No per-second extraction or batching.
after: Added batch observation mode (qwen_batch_observe=true): regions are split into sub-batches of max N seconds (qwen_max_seconds_per_batch, default 30), each batch extracts exactly 1 frame per second and makes a separate API call. Evidence from all batches is merged with batch-indexed headers. Three new methods: _split_regions_into_batches(), _infer_single_batch(), _merge_batch_evidence(). Two new config fields: qwen_batch_observe (bool), qwen_max_seconds_per_batch (int). Factory function updated to pass new fields.
why: Sports video analysis requires per-second frame coverage to capture fast actions. Splitting into batches keeps each API call within Qwen's context limits while ensuring no second is skipped. The planner identifies regions of interest, and batch observation thoroughly covers each region at 1fps.
verification: Syntax check passed. Unit tests for batch splitting (6 cases including edge cases) and evidence merging all passed. Config loading with new fields verified.
recovery_notes: Backward compatible: qwen_batch_observe defaults to false. Existing behavior unchanged unless explicitly enabled. To revert: remove 2 fields from config.py, remove 3 methods and batch dispatch block from qwen_client.py, remove 2 fields from factory function.
